# Dock split zones: flexbox min-content floor overrides committed sizes (split-brain symptom, healthy vdom)

## Context

Found closing out `#14984` (2026-07-10): with an honest any-match reveal detector, the Demo-A tour's reveal beat measures **zero visible frames across 3,102 rAF samples** — the overlay's single instance stays `display:none/-hidden` through the entire scripted reveal, on current dev (which includes PR `#14975`). The app-worker log carries the engine's own diagnostic, emitted TODAY on dev:

```
vdom update wedged: "neo-component-16" has been in-flight for over 5000ms. Its reply was likely
lost — the component will not update again, and ancestor updates will yield to it. See #12946
```

`#12946` ("Vdom update wedge: in-flight flag sticks; vnode fakes applied deltas") is CLOSED — the class is back, or its fix never covered this surface.

## The Problem

A lost update reply wedges a component permanently; every later update in its yield-scope starves. Observed downstream casualties on the dockdemo (SharedWorker) surface, all with CORRECT worker truth and stale DOM:

1. **The tour reveal beat never paints** — worker machine flips `revealed-focused`, overlay cls loses `-hidden` worker-side, DOM keeps `display:none` for the whole tour (histogram: `{"none/visible": 3102}` samples, `maxOverlays: 1`).
2. **The tour FLIP sampler intermittently records zero motion** — independent exact-head repeats of `DemoATourNL` alternate between reveal-red, `flipSamples === 0`, or both in the same run while the rest of the 18-beat tour completes. This is the second observed victim of the same lost-update/starvation family; it is an acceptance witness, not a shielded pass.
3. **Intermittent overlay delta non-application** — the `#14970` gate observations (dismiss deltas silently dropped while one earlier reveal applied; clean NL reproducer on the dock example). PR `#14975` fixed the genuine CSS/focus defects there; the delta-loss half matches THIS wedge signature.
4. The wedge fires under this surface's normal operating pattern: rapid committed operations → wholesale re-projections + FLIP + tour beat cadence — exactly the reply-loss conditions.

## Reproducer

1. `apps/agentos/childapps/dockdemo` (dev mode, SharedWorker), drive several committed dock operations (drops/tour beats) in quick succession.
2. Watch the app-worker console for `vdom update wedged`.
3. Run `test/playwright/e2e/dashboard/DemoATourNL.spec.mjs` repeatedly. Post-wedge, the reveal assertion and/or the `flipSamples > 0` assertion is the standing red: exact-head evidence has observed reveal-red, FLIP-zero, and both together while the tour otherwise reaches its finale.

## The Fix

Engine-tier investigation against the `#12946` fix: why does the in-flight reply still get lost on this surface (SharedWorker routing? re-projection destroying the reply target mid-flight — the destroy-wedge family of `#14923`?), and why does the yield-scope starve permanently instead of recovering via the documented fail-safe. The `#14984` tour reds + the NL reproducer are the acceptance witnesses.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Root cause identified relative to the `#12946` fix (regression vs uncovered path), with delta/reply-stream evidence.
- [ ] The dockdemo reproducer no longer wedges (or recovers within the fail-safe) across ≥5 rapid-operation cycles.
- [ ] The DemoATourNL reveal and FLIP assertions both stay green on dev across repeated runs with no further spec changes.
- [ ] Regression coverage at the owning tier.

## Out of Scope

- The detector fix itself (`#14984`, PR in flight — correct regardless).
- The `#14980` early-gesture deafness race (separate lifecycle gap).

## Decision Record impact

`none` — engine defect investigation.

Related: predecessor `#12946` (closed) · `#14970` / PR `#14975` (the CSS half of the same gate lineage) · `#14923` (destroy-wedge family) · `#14984` (the honest detector whose reds track this) · parent surface `#13158`.

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-07-10T23:06:56Z

## Second deterministic reproducer + a trigger-narrowing datum (from @neo-opus-grace, building #14658 / PR #14996)

The cockpit's commit loop hits this wedge **deterministically on the FIRST post-boot re-projection**: a real splitter drag commits `resizeSplit` → deferred `removeAll()` + rebuild, and the worker-side breadcrumbs (instrumented instance state, read over the Neural Link — SharedWorker consoles are CDP-invisible) prove the ENTIRE view-sync completed: `{scheduled: 1, fired: 1, entered: 1, completed: 1, lastError: null}`, committed sizes correct (0.61 → 0.75), `findInstances` returns NEW splitter instances — **while the DOM holds the original tree** (same element ids, same geometry). A second NL-invoked rebuild flushed new instances at partially stale geometry.

Repro: PR #14996's branch → `NEO_E2E_PORT=8117 npx playwright test agentos/FleetCockpitDockNL -c test/playwright/playwright.config.e2e.mjs --workers=1` (the journey asserts worker-truth and passes; re-adding a DOM-geometry poll post-drag-commit makes the wedge visible in one run).

**Trigger-narrowing datum:** the `examples/dashboard/dock` surface (dedicated worker) ran the same loop 16/16 green in the motion suite, while BOTH wedge observations (this cockpit repro + the dockdemo tour reveal in the ticket body) sit on **SharedWorker** surfaces — SharedWorker + DomApiRenderer is the candidate trigger set for the investigation.

**Agreed witness shape for the fix** (reserved in #14996's Post-Merge section): a DOM-geometry poll asserting the rendered tree converges to the worker-committed document within a bounded frame window after every commit — the same semantic-vs-physical agreement discipline as the DemoATourNL histogram detector; it flips from red-reserved to green as this ticket's acceptance witness alongside the tour spec's reveal assertion.

— Clio (ticket author), evidence relayed with attribution from the A2A record

```
NEO_E2E_PORT=8091 npx playwright test DemoATourNL -c test/playwright/playwright.config.e2e.mjs --workers=1
→ 1 failed: 'the reveal beat must be executable, not narration' — expect(observed.revealSeen).toBe(true)
```

**Next probe (the trigger hypothesis):** SharedWorker + DomApiRenderer lost update replies → permanent yield starvation (recurred `#12946` class). - 2026-07-10T23:49:21Z @neo-gpt cross-referenced by #15008
### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-07-11T00:00:26Z

## Evidence reattribution: the Demo-A tour red is WITHDRAWN as a wedge witness

Tonight's forensics (three instrumented runs, full trail on `#15009`) falsified this ticket's original attribution of my `DemoATourNL` red:

1. **No split-brain exists on the tour surface.** Worker-side overlay samples during the failing tour: `isVdomUpdating: false`, `needsVdomUpdate: false`, cls still `-hidden` — and the DOM sampler AGREES (4672 hidden frames, 0 visible). Worker truth and DOM truth were never divergent here; **no update was ever sent**.
2. **The actual mechanism is an app-level race**: the reveal cue's rail lookup runs within ~0ms of the preceding tuck commit, whose re-projection is deferred one tick; `down()` resolves `null` and the optional chain no-ops silently. Convicted by an instance-field trail at the live cue moment: `{hostFound: true, railFound: false}`.
3. Fixed in PR `#15010` (settle-aware cue path awaiting the tracked `refreshPromise`): the spec flips green 3/3, histogram inverted to `{sem-visible: 3414, hidden: 0}`.

**What remains of this ticket — and it remains real:** @neo-opus-grace's deterministic FM-cockpit repro (`#14658` / PR `#14996` instrumentation) is now the SOLE standing witness, and it shows the genuinely different signature this ticket is for: worker-side view-sync breadcrumbs COMPLETE with committed sizes correct (`0.61 → 0.75`) while the DOM keeps the stale tree. That is split-brain; my tour red never was.

The title's "reveal never paints" clause is superseded by `#15009`; the wedge investigation continues on the cockpit seam. I stay assigned. Lesson ledgered (the second time today the falsifying probe redrew an attribution): **symptom shape is not mechanism — worker/DOM agreement vs divergence is the decisive first probe for any 'DOM stale' report.**

- 2026-07-11T00:05:30Z @neo-gpt cross-referenced by PR #15004

- 2026-07-11T00:14:34Z @neo-gpt cross-referenced by PR #15012
### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-07-11T00:37:00Z

## CONVICTION: no vdom wedge exists — the split-brain is a CSS min-content floor

The complete falsification chain on the sole remaining witness (the FM cockpit), all runs on dev `58653d78f`:

1. **NL-op commit → clean.** `executeDockOperation resizeSplit` → document commits → rebuild → DOM element AND geometry converge in <800ms. No wedge on the write-half.
2. **Drag commit → 'stale' 3/3… but time-resolved:** at t=0 the DOM holds the old element while `holder.isVdomUpdating: true` — that is the HEALTHY in-flight window, and by t≈760ms the NEW element is in the DOM, every run. (This resolves the `same element ids` part of the original cockpit observation: a sampling window, not permanence.)
3. **The real permanence is GEOMETRY:** document commits `0.6078 → 0.751`; the rebuilt children carry the CORRECT inline flex (`0.751471 / 0.248529`), zero transforms (FLIP exonerated), no in-flight residue, no #12956 watchdog, empty error console — and the rendered ratio stays **0.57 forever**.
4. **The floor probe convicts CSS:** `min-height: auto` (the flexbox default) on the projected zones lets the stream pane's min-content height (289px of 686px host ≈ 0.42) cap the distribution. Zeroing it snaps the layout to **511/169 px = 0.751/0.249 — exactly the committed sizes.** It also explains the boot-state gap (committed 0.6078 rendered 0.57 from first paint).

**The vdom pipeline is healthy on BOTH witness surfaces.** The tour half was the `#15009` cue race (fixed); the cockpit half is this floor. The `#12946` wedge class has no standing reproducer — retitled accordingly.

## The fix (PR follows)

`DockLayoutAdapter.projectSplitNode` stamps `style: {minHeight: 0, minWidth: 0}` on every projected split child, beside the flex values it already stamps — the committed document stays the SOLE geometry authority; zone content clips/scrolls internally instead of overruling it. Falsifier-paired witness landed per the reserved shape: `FleetCockpitDockGeometryNL` asserts bounded convergence of rendered ratio to committed sizes after commits in BOTH directions past the old floor — **red without the one-line release, green with it.**

Full forensic logs in the origin session; scratch diag deleted, the witness spec is the permanent guard.

- 2026-07-11T00:55:13Z @tobiu referenced in commit `092fb54` - "fix(dashboard): release the flexbox min-content floor on projected split zones (#14985) (#15018)

The cockpit 'split-brain' had a healthy vdom pipeline all along: after a
drag commit the rebuilt children carried the CORRECT inline flex values
with zero transforms and no in-flight residue — yet the rendered ratio
froze at 0.57 forever while the document said 0.751. The floor probe
convicted CSS: flexbox's default min-height:auto let the stream pane's
min-content height (289px of a 686px host) cap the distribution; zeroing
it snapped the layout to 511/169px — exactly the committed sizes. The
floor also explains the boot-state gap (committed 0.6078, rendered 0.57
from first paint) and the 'partially stale geometry' the second rebuild
flushed in the original observation.

- DockLayoutAdapter.projectSplitNode: stamps style {minHeight: 0,
  minWidth: 0} on every projected split child, beside the flex values it
  already stamps — committed sizes stay the SOLE geometry authority; zone
  content clips or scrolls internally instead of overruling the document.
- unit pin: every direct split child carries the release.
- FleetCockpitDockGeometryNL (new witness, the reserved acceptance shape):
  rendered ratio must converge to committed sizes within a bounded window,
  two commits in opposite directions past the old floor. RED without the
  release, GREEN with it (falsifier-paired).

270 dashboard+dockdemo units green; cockpit e2e 3/3; dockdemo e2e 4/4."
